message:
Simplify release trigger workflow

Remove manual release inputs from trigger-release.yaml so the
repo uses the default release flow through the reusable
workflow. Also fix the release publish tests to disable local
commit and tag signing explicitly, so they pass when the
workflow enables global GPG signing.

files.patch:
@@ -3,41 +3,6 @@ name: Release
 on:
   workflow_dispatch:
     inputs:
-      bump:
-        description: |
-          Version bump type
-
-          Use `auto` to infer from unreleased changelog entries. Leave this as
-          `auto` when providing an explicit version below.
-        required: false
-        # workflow_dispatch choice inputs cannot represent an unset value.
-        # `auto` is the explicit sentinel for "infer bump".
-        default: auto
-        type: choice
-        options:
-          - auto
-          - minor
-          - patch
-          - major
-      version:
-        description: |
-          Explicit stable release version (for example `v1.2.3`)
-
-          Prefer `rc: true` for release candidates. Use `version` only for an
-          exact stable release or to override the stable base explicitly.
-        required: false
-        type: string
-      rc:
-        description: |
-          Create or continue the release-candidate series for the resolved
-          stable version.
-
-          When enabled, omit `version` to infer the RC base from changelog
-          entries, or provide a stable `version` like `v1.2.3` to override the
-          base explicitly.
-        required: false
-        default: false
-        type: boolean
       intro:
         description: |
           Intro of the release
@@ -81,14 +46,12 @@ jobs:
           fi
 
   release:
+    name: Create GitHub release
     needs: validate-release-config
     permissions:
       contents: write
     uses: ./.github/workflows/release.yaml
     with:
-      bump: ${{ inputs.bump }}
-      version: ${{ inputs.version }}
-      rc: ${{ inputs.rc }}
       intro: ${{ inputs.intro }}
       title: ${{ inputs.title }}
       git-add-paths: pyproject.toml uv.lock

@@ -3784,7 +3784,7 @@ def test_release_publish_creates_git_tag(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
         check=True,
     )
     subprocess.run(
-        ["git", "config", "commit.gpgsign", "false"],
+        ["git", "config", "tag.gpgsign", "false"],
         cwd=project_dir,
         check=True,
     )
@@ -3916,6 +3916,16 @@ def test_release_publish_skips_existing_tag(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
         cwd=project_dir,
         check=True,
     )
+    subprocess.run(
+        ["git", "config", "commit.gpgsign", "false"],
+        cwd=project_dir,
+        check=True,
+    )
+    subprocess.run(
+        ["git", "config", "tag.gpgsign", "false"],
+        cwd=project_dir,
+        check=True,
+    )
     remote_dir = tmp_path / "remote.git"
     subprocess.run(
         ["git", "init", "--bare", str(remote_dir)],
